## Summary

Draft implementation plan for Revise patch lifecycle and safety.

This PR must be implemented only after PR #849 is merged.

## Goal

Add safe preview/apply behavior for Revise patches without destructive manuscript writes.

A/B/C/custom are candidate patches.

No patch applies without preview.
No patch applies destructively.
No patch applies if the source anchor has changed.
Applying a patch creates a new manuscript version and an audit record.

## Scope

- Patch preview for A/B/C/custom.
- Patch apply only after preview.
- Separate decision status from application status.
- Preserve revision_operation for A/B/C/custom.
- Store source_text_snapshot and source_text_hash.
- Detect source conflict before apply.
- Create a new manuscript version on apply.
- Persist audit records for preview/apply/conflict/failure.

## Out of scope

- No prompt changes.
- No evaluation pipeline changes.
- No TrustedPath changes.
- No E2E proof; that belongs in the next PR.
- No destructive overwrite path.

## MVP apply-enabled operations

- replace_selected_passage
- insert_before_selected_passage
- insert_after_selected_passage
- compress_selected_passage

## High-risk operations

These must remain blocked or preview-only until later hardening:

- rewrite_multi_paragraph_span
- delete_selected_passage
- merge_paragraphs
- reorder_within_section

## Acceptance criteria

- Preview succeeds for replacement and insertion operations.
- Apply creates a new manuscript version.
- Original manuscript version remains unchanged.
- Audit record is written.
- Custom text follows the same preview/apply path.
- Empty or meta custom text is rejected.
- Source hash mismatch blocks apply.
- High-risk operations are blocked or preview-only.
